text:
giving the details of the bank account as well as the date and time 
of the withdrawal of the said amount which was given to the accused 
and also the date and time of the payment made to the accused, 
including the date and time of receiving of the cheque, which has 
not been done in the present case. Pausing here, such presumption 
on the complainant, by the High Court, appears to be erroneous. 
The onus is not on the complainant at the threshold to prove his 
capacity/financial wherewithal to make the payment in discharge of 
which the cheque is alleged to have been issued in his favour. Only 
if an objection is raised that the complainant was not in a financial 
position to pay the amount so claimed by him to have been given 
as a loan to the accused, only then the complainant would have to 
bring before the Court cogent material to indicate that he had the 
financial capacity and had actually advanced the amount in question
